title: Vue.js Best Practices Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of modularity, Composition API vs Options API, and performance (v-if/v-for).

questions.question: Which and/or what is the Single Responsibility Principle for Vue components?
options: A component should only handle one thing and handle it well.
A component must have at least 500 lines of code.
A component can only be used once in an app.

questions.question: Which API is generally preferred for building more professional and complex Vue 3 applications?
Which of the following is true about using v-if and v-for on the same element?
options: It's a great practice for performance.
It's generally discouraged because v-for has a higher priority and will evaluate the condition for every item.

questions.question: What is the recommended alternative for using v-if on a list?
options: Filter the list in a computed property before rendering it with v-for.
Only write formatting logic in HTML comments.

questions.question: What is the recommended naming convention for Vue components?
